programmes.show_name: The Secret World of Richard Nixon
programmes.show_description: This episode reveals Nixon's conspiracy to sabotage the 1968 peace talks with Vietnam in order to extend the war and allow a Nixon election victory later that same year. Ep2
A look at the massive part that aircraft played in the Vietnam War with combat footage filmed over North and South Vietnam and the part the war played in shaping many of the weapons used today.
One of the hotel's showers appears inexplicably occupied baffling staff and guests alike, and an Evangelical Christian couple check in. S1 E6
Explore the events which led to the Second World War - from Germany's defeat in 1918 to Hitler's rise to power - and chart the quick successes of Nazi forces up to the surrender of France in 1940. E1
The Battle of Stalingrad was the bloodiest and most destructive battle of the Second World War, taking countless lives and obliterating an ideologically and industrially important Soviet city. E1
We explore the way in which British and American aircraft designers were able to close the gap with the technologically more advanced German and Japanese aircraft of the Second World War.
Counterstrikes can take many forms, and history is full of examples where a numerically inferior army has turned the tide of battle by a brilliant execution of a counterstrike. S1 E5
The Resistance asked for help in late 1943 as some of their top operatives were being held in Amiens prison and needed rescuing from facing interrogation, torture and execution. Ep2: Prison Busters
...You? An airplane shot down over Kigali in Rwanda in 1994 was a single act of terrorism that ignited ethnic tensions between the Hutus and the Tutsis and triggered 100 days of killing.

Six men, including ex-England rugby pro James Forrester, re-enact a mission to discover the true feat of Operation Jaywick, the attack by 14 British and Australian commandos on Japanese ships in WWII.
During 1938, Hitler annexed Austria and the German speaking areas of Czechoslovakia into the Third Reich.
Experience the chaos and turmoil of World War One on a journey through the trenches of France where American soldiers fought alongside their European allies to achieve victory.
Billy cannot get work due to his socialist views, and Tom befriends a Russian Jew hoping to travel to Palestine and looking for Jack who he says can help him. S2 E4: Roubles for the Promised Land
With typical aplomb, Arthur announces the launch of his Daley Into Space business, to flog some satellite dishes.
Needing money to open a mine free of George's influence, Ross strikes a deal with a local smuggler. Starring Aidan Turner. S2 E4
Sam encounters young Tony Crane, a villain he put away in 2006. He realises he is faced with the chance every copper dreams about - stopping the killer before he kills. Starring John Simm. S2 E1
As Christmas approaches Alf Garnett's problems increase - but things are eventually resolved by Caribbean magic. In Sickness and in Health Christmas Special 1986

World War One, also known as the Great War, started in 1914 after the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria; his murder catapulted a war across Europe that lasted until 1918.
Relationships come under the spotlight at the Damson Dene as Nadine and Christian try to repair their marriage; Bob and Jeanie celebrate their 50th year together. S1 E7
After the evacuation at Dunkirk, Britain had lost not only men, but also heavy weaponry, warships, and aircraft. Britain alone faced Germany across many fronts, including Rommel in North Africa. E2
1944 would be a disastrous year for German forces in the East - as the Soviet army went on the offensive and a second front opened, weary German troops were forced to retreat to Berlin. E2
We explore all the major battles of the Second World War, from the early defeats in Norway and France to the military successes at El Alamein and Tunisia and the surrender of the Nazis in 1945.
Examining the history of war tactics. We explore how blockades have the ability to destroy or at least damage a country's ability to wage war. S1 E6: Blockade
As Britain began to strike Nazi Germany, it was soon suffering losses from hi-tech air defences; in 1941, British paratroops were sent to seize a German radar system which needed to be cracked.

programmes.show_name: Idi Amin Terror: Where Were You?
programmes.show_description: The brutal reign of one of the 20th century's most notorious and bloodiest dictators, Idi Amin, began in 1971 when he seized control beginning a reign of torture, terror and murder of 300,000 people.
We explore the overlap between naval crafts and aircraft in a number of wars and how aircraft carriers played a pivotal role by bridging the mobility of planes with the buoyant fortresses of the sea.
Adolf Hitler loved cars. But it was travelling by car through crowds, which could easily harbor assassins that he was at his most vulnerable - and his bodyguard at their most alert. Ep 8
As the greatest loss of warships in history and the last casualties of WWI, the sinking at Scapa Flow was a monumental decision with consequences for European history that still reverberate today.
Les Mallow wants Jack to help him expose a scandal and help to secure him a place on the council; he is prepared to use any means to secure Jack's help. S2 E5: Some Bulbs to Keep the Garden Bright
The Winchester Club is faced with permanent closure when one of Arthur's money-making schemes backfires badly, but Ray has an idea, a charity football match. S9 E9: Last Orders at the Winchester
Francis secures a lenient sentence for a smuggler, but Ross suspects an informer; elsewhere, Elizabeth admits feelings for Ross while Warleggan gains power. S2 E5
Armed robberies lead the CID unit to prison, to escort safe-cracker Dickie Fingers for interview. They are set upon by a gang determined to get their hands on Fingers. Starring John Simm. S2 E2
